channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: WhatsApp vs. Apple Messages: Which is the better messaging app in 2024?
item.description: Messaging apps are one of the most frequently used apps on our phones. There’s probably not a day that goes by when most people don’t hear a ding alerting them to a new text. There’s also no shortage of messaging apps.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Parallels debuts AI virtual machine platform that bundles 14 tools including small language models — and promises faster performance on Windows and Linux
item.description: Parallels Desktop 20 introduces advanced AI features, including a secure AI-ready virtual machine. A new Enterprise Edition offers management tools and security updates, while compatibility with macOS Sequoia and Windows 11 ensures flexibility.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Is the stock market in an AI bubble?
item.description: When it comes to AI investments, “a lot of money is going to get wasted,” MIT professor Daron Acemoglu told Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navigating the federal AI frontier: Top priorities for incoming chief AI officers
item.description: There are ten key focus areas for current and prospective agency CAIOs to consider to ensure and maximize the benefits and minimize the risks of AI. The post Navigating the federal AI frontier: Top priorities for incoming chief AI officers first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Social media suspensions may be linked to misinformation, not platform bias: Study
item.description: Social media platforms’ suspensions of accounts may not be rooted in political biases, but rather certain political groups’ tendency to share misinformation, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: I’m addicted to TikTok. I’m begging the government to ban it
item.description: I downloaded TikTok back in 2020, by which point it had moved from cringe teen fad to pandemic must-have. Since then, I’ve seen my attention span fracture and metastasize. The endless scroll subsumed me, along with some 48 million others in my Gen Z cohort.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Open AI is testing a new interface for ChatGPT — ‘Canvas’
item.description: Open AI has unveiled Canvas, a new ChatGPT interface specifically crafted for developers that is now available in beta. Canvas has been developed using GPT-4o and makes it possible, among other things, to use a separate window for code.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: NSA’s Kristina Walter on exposing ‘sophisticated’ cyber adversaries
item.description: The Cybersecurity Collaboration Center director pursues whole-of-government approach to helping agencies and industry tackle global cyberthreats. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: NSA’s Kristina Walter on exposing ‘sophisticated’ cyber adversaries first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: The chief of the Shawnee Tribe grew up playing video games, including hundreds of hours colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier’s Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: Graham Lee Brewer And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news if a law passes forcing it to pay for content
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Google said Friday it will stop linking to New Zealand news content and will reverse its support of local media outlets if the government passes a law forcing tech companies to pay for articles displayed on their platforms.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Los 12 desastres más famosos de la IA
item.description: En 2017, The Economist declaró que los datos, en lugar del petróleo, se habían convertido en el recurso más valioso del mundo. El estribillo se ha repetido desde entonces. Organizaciones de todos los sectores han estado invirtiendo, y siguen invirtiendo fuertemente, en datos y analítica.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AWS, 아마존 베드록 국내 정식 출시 “서울 리전에서 지원”
item.description: 아마존웹서비스(이하 AWS)가 3일 AWS 아시아 태평양(서울) 리전의 아마존 베드록(Amazon Bedrock)을 정식 출시한다고 발표했다. 아마존 베드록은 생성형 인공지능(AI) 애플리케이션을 구축하고 확장하려는 사용자가 특정 용도에 적합한 고성능 파운데이션 모델(FM) 및 거대언어모델(LLM)을 쉽게 찾고 접근할 수 있도록 하는 서비스다. AWS는 2023년부터 일부 지역을 시작으로 해당 서비스를 제공해오고 있다.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why OpenAI needs another $6.6 billion in VC money
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: The EU wants to know more about social media algorithms
item.description: Via the Digital Services Regulation (DSA), the European Commission has requested information from Youtube, Snapchat and Tiktok about which parameters their algorithms use to recommend social media content to users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How data brokers are fueling elder fraud in America
item.description: Criminals are increasingly turning to data brokers to provide access to private information that can be used to improve their scamming schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Future of Generative AI: OpenAI's Latest Updates to Catch Up with Rivals
item.description: OpenAI releases updates to its API to ease generative AI-based application development, including model distillation and prompt caching capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google, it’s time to kill CAPTCHAS
item.description: Are you a robot? Google really, really wants to know. The answer to this question is demanded of web users 200 million times a day via CAPTCHAs — “Completely Automated Public Turing test to tell Computers and Humans Apart,” a system owned and operated by Google.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spider lovers scurry to Colorado town in search of mating tarantulas and community
item.description: It’s tarantula mating season on the Colorado plains, when male spiders scurry out of their burrows in search of a mate.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Peipert And Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CISA faces challenges to sharing cyber threat information
item.description: The IG said CISA made some good improvements to the Automated Indicator Sharing program in recent years. The post CISA faces challenges to sharing cyber threat information first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: What to expect from Apple this October and beyond
item.description: The Apple product machine continues to whirr, with a host of new products, including Macs, iPads, and iPhone SE, expected to appear this fall.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Docker tutorial: Get started with Docker
item.description: Containers are a lightweight way to make application workloads portable, like a virtual machine but without the overhead and bulk typically associated with VMs. With containers, you can package apps and services and move them freely between physical, virtual, or cloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why determining the cost of cloud services cuts across an entire agency
item.description: Do you know your true cloud costs? The post Why determining the cost of cloud services cuts across an entire agency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Two good Visual Studio Code alternatives
item.description: The conductor of my choir famously tells us singers, “I only want everything all the time.” Well, as a developer, my mantra for code editors and IDEs (integrated development environments) is exactly that: I only want everything all the time.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Large language models hallucinating non-existent developer packages could fuel supply chain attacks
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) have a serious “package hallucination” problem that could lead to a wave of maliciously-coded packages in the supply chain, researchers have discovered in one of the largest and most in-depth ever studies to investigate the problem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile service outages reported by users
item.description: People in multiple cities posted about issues they were having, with many saying their phones were in "SOS" mode.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Chinese EV tech ban not an overreaction: National security experts
item.description: A tech ban on EVs will limit the Chinese government from accessing data on American drivers, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation
